title: Anacamptis × heraclea
taxonomy: Anacamptis × heraclea is a hybrid of Anacamptis morio and Orchis palustris, both of which belong to the family Orchidaceae. This classification puts it in the genus Anacamptis and the family Orchidaceae. As a hybrid, it is often classified as a subspecies of Anacamptis morio or considered a distinct species within the genus. The classification of Anacamptis × heraclea can vary depending on the source and the taxonomic system used. It is generally accepted as a rare and unique orchid species.

intro: Anacamptis × heraclea, also known as Heraclea's orchid, is a hybrid plant species within the Orchidaceae family, resulting from the cross between Anacamptis pyramidalis and Anacamptis coriophora. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Anacamptis × heraclea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Anacamptis × heraclea is often used in preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
